description: Tokenization replaces sensitive data with non-sensitive tokens while masking obscures data for display purposes. Both reduce data exposure without eliminating utility.

architecture.diagram:
[Application] --request--> [Tokenization Service]
                              |
                        [Token Vault]
                     (token <-> original)
                              |
                     [HSM / Key Store]
                              |
[De-tokenization] <--authorized request-- [Authorized Service]

tokenizationTypes.description: Token-to-data mapping stored in encrypted vault database
tokenizationTypes.pros: No mathematical relationship between token and data
tokenizationTypes.cons: Vault is single point of failure, scalability challenges
Mathematically reversible if key is compromised

tokenizationTypes.description: Token maintains same format/length as original data (e.g., 16-digit PAN stays 16 digits)
tokenizationTypes.pros: No schema changes needed, application compatibility

maskingTypes.description: Irreversible masking applied to copies of data for non-production use
Real-time masking applied at query level based on user role
maskingTypes.useCase: Production data access control, helpdesk views

maskingTypes.description: Masking applied during data movement (ETL, replication)
maskingTypes.useCase: Data warehouse loading, cross-environment data transfer

vendorLandscape.strengths: FPE tokenization, integrated with HPE ecosystem
Test data management, virtual data masking

malaysianContext.bnmRmit: BNM RMiT S10.52 requires masking of sensitive data in non-production environments. Tokenization is accepted as a PCI DSS scope reduction mechanism.
